host: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (gfx1100/RDNA3) via HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1, TheRock ROCm

methodology.summary: Aggregate (total across the batch) and per-sequence decode tok/s vs number of concurrent decode sequences c, fixed 512/128 per sequence. Self-consistent within each path; c=1 and c>1 use different harnesses (noted) so read aggregate scaling, not an exact cross-harness ratio at c=1->2.
methodology.c1: scripts/qwen35_paro_bench.py --graph-replay-decode -> throughput.warmed_decode_tok_s (single-sequence, real generate-time HIP graph replay)
methodology.cN: scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py --batch-size N -> measurements.decode_tok_s_aggregate / decode_tok_s_per_request (scheduler-owned compact native prefill + step_batch_native)

correctness_gate: Native c>1 decode is gated by native_batch_vs_independent_c1 generated-token equality (0 mismatches at c2/c4/c8) and the per-kernel CPU-reference / primitive correctness gates; this is a throughput measurement, not a retained performance claim (performance_claim=false).
notes: Aggregate decode tok/s scales with concurrency: c1 133.8 -> c2 131.1 (0.98x) -> c4 181.6 (1.36x) -> c8 225.9 (1.69x).

c=2 aggregate sits slightly below c=1: the c>1 native path is dispatch-bound at this small context and c=2 does not yet amortize per-step host/device dispatch over enough rows (see docs/CONCURRENCY.md C3.0b/C3.0c; graph replay removes residual host-side launch latency only, dispatch-count reduction is the remaining lever).
Per-sequence tok/s falls as expected with more concurrent rows (133.8 / 65.5 / 45.4 / 28.2).
llama.cpp and vLLM concurrency baselines are not yet measured on this host; columns are TBD.
